name: Romanian Deadlift
resistance_profile: Tension peaks at the stretched (bottom) position with hips flexed — strong lengthened loading of the hamstrings.
rom_notes: Lower until you feel a strong hamstring stretch without lumbar rounding; range is set by hamstring flexibility, not the floor.
cues: Push the hips back, minimal knee bend
Keep the bar close, dragging up the legs
execution_steps: Stand holding a barbell at your thighs, feet hip-width, knees softly bent.
Push your hips back, keeping the bar dragging close along your legs.
Lower until you feel a strong hamstring stretch without rounding your back.
Drive your hips forward to lockout, squeezing your glutes.
good_when: You want a heavily loadable, stretch-biased hamstring builder.
You want to train hamstrings, glutes, and erectors together in one lift.
bad_when: You need a low-fatigue isolation option (use a leg curl).
You lack the hip mobility or back control to hinge without rounding (start with the dumbbell RDL).
common_errors: Rounding the lower back to reach the floor
progressions: Add a pause in the stretched position
